title: Filipino Bryndza Cheese Menudo with Citrusy Spring Twist
description: A Filipino-style menudo dish made with bryndza cheese, featuring a citrusy and springy twist, perfect for a low-glycemic-friendly brunch option.
instructions: In a large pan, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the mixed vegetables, chicken broth, orange juice, fish sauce, salt, and black pepper. Stir well to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
In a separate pan, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble until cooked through.
Add the cooked eggs to the vegetable mixture and stir well to combine.
Add the cooked rice to the pan and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes, until the rice is well coated with the vegetable mixture.
Finally, crumble the bryndza cheese on top of the menudo and stir-fry for another minute, until the cheese is melted and well combined.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Wrap each serving in a large leaf wrapper and steam for 5 minutes to serve.
